rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible or properly declared in the same annex scope.
When AGREE diagnostics report type errors, trace and resolve the first invalid identifier or helper declaration on the same line.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to missing references should be resolved by correcting or removing the invalid AGREE clause.
AGREE contracts must only reference visible identifiers or helpers declared in the same annex scope.
AGREE syntax errors often stem from unresolved identifiers or invalid type expressions in guarantees or assumptions.
Minimal repair involves fixing only the root cause of a validator error without altering unrelated architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component implementation annexes are used for bindings and assignments valid in that scope.
Ensure all identifiers in a guarantee or assumption are either visible features or declared helpers in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors instead of resolving the root reference issue.
Introducing invalid or unresolved identifiers in AGREE contracts without ensuring their visibility or declaration.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees
